title: Operators: Delete & Change
intro: Operators combine with motions: `d` deletes, `c` changes (deletes and enters Insert mode). `dw` deletes a word, `dd` deletes a whole line, `D` deletes to the end of the line. Text objects like `aw` (a word) work from anywhere inside the word: `daw`, `ciw`.

exercises.instruction: The cursor is on the word `bug`. Delete it (and the space after it) with `dw`.
hints: `d` + `w` deletes from the cursor to the start of the next word.

exercises.instruction: Delete the entire middle line with `dd`.
hints: Press `d` twice to delete the whole current line.

exercises.instruction: The cursor is on the comment. Delete from here to the end of the line with `D`.
exercises.initialCode: let debug = true; // delete this comment

exercises.instruction: The cursor is in the middle of `brown`. Delete the whole word with `daw` — no need to move to its start.
hints: `aw` is the text object "a word" — it works from anywhere inside the word.

exercises.instruction: Rename the variable: change the word `foo` to `count` using `ciw`, type the new name, then press `Esc`.
hints: `ciw` deletes the inner word and drops you into Insert mode.
